summary:
Electrophoretic analysis generally reveals no change in hemoglobin F (HbF), while a mild reduction may occur in hemoglobin A2 (HbA2). The correct answer is C. 

This summary captures the essential aspects regarding both HbF and HbA2 as they relate specifically to detecting alpha-thalassemia through electrophoresis.
